message.content:
Event Triggered Execution involves adversaries leveraging system mechanisms that trigger execution based on specific events to establish persistence or elevate privileges. These mechanisms can monitor and respond to events such as logons, user activities, or specific application executions. In cloud environments, similar functions and services can be exploited. Adversaries may create or modify event triggers to execute malicious code whenever the event occurs, maintaining persistent access. This technique can also be used to escalate privileges by executing code under higher-permission accounts like SYSTEM or service accounts.

Potential impacts include unauthorized persistent access and privilege escalation, leading to further system compromise. Common exploitation methods include modifying system event triggers, abusing scheduled tasks, and leveraging cloud event functions.

Mitigation strategies include monitoring and auditing event trigger configurations, employing principle of least privilege, and using endpoint detection and response (EDR) tools to detect abnormal event trigger modifications. Regularly reviewing and securing system and cloud event configurations can also help prevent exploitation.
